methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $88M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Lohum Cleantech is a Series C-stage climate and energy company that has raised $87.7M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public entity operating in the same sector and stage, including peers such as KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

Two triangulation methods are applied. The stage-progression method uses total capital raised ($88M) as a proxy base value and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $149M. The peer-median method benchmarks against three same-sector, same-stage comparables: KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M). The median across these three peers is $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $2.0B.

The triangulated analysis produces a low estimate of $127M, a median of $575M, and a high of $1.1B. The substantial spread between methods reflects material uncertainty in whether Lohum's operational maturity and capital efficiency align more closely with stage-progression norms or with the higher valuations observed in the peer set. Key sensitivities include market-rate assumptions for climate-tech deployment capital and the durability of peer valuations in a volatile sector.
